samples.question: I missed the normal course registration day because I was sick. Can I still register or change my courses, and what part of the procedure should I pay attention to?
samples.answer_text: If you missed the normal course registration day because you were sick, you should complete your registration during the registration change period [3], [4], [5]. After the registration change period ends, the registration will not be accepted for any reason [3], [4], [5]. If you do not complete the registration during the designated period, you must take a leave of absence or withdraw from the university [3], [4], [5]. When making a registration change, make sure to click the "SAVE" button to complete the procedure, as the process is not finished unless this button is clicked [3], [4], [5].
samples.gold_answer: If you missed Registration Day, you should complete Late Registration during the registration change period; after that period, no registration is accepted for any reason. Complete the procedure with the SAVE button, confirm your added/cancelled courses in Registration Results, and receive your advisor's interview/online approval. Late Registration requires the 3,000 yen fee before advisor approval.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly states the late-registration window, no-registration-after-period rule, SAVE/results checks, advisor approval, and fee.
retrieved_passage_titles: ehandbook - November 28, 2023 Important Information from Educational Affairs Group
ehandbook - August 26, 2025 Important Information from Educational Affairs Group
ehandbook - August 25, 2023 Important Information from Educational Affairs Group
samples.human_judge_notes: Omits advisor approval and the late-registration fee.

samples.question: If I want to withdraw from a course after seeing my workload, what rules or deadlines should I check before I make the decision?
samples.answer_text: To withdraw from a course, you should check the following: * **Withdrawal Type:** Withdrawal means withdrawing all course registration for an ongoing term, and it is not possible to withdraw course registration partially [1], [3]. * **Reason for Withdrawal:** If you cannot continue your studies for an ongoing term due to illness or an unavoidable reason recognized by the University, permission for Registration Withdrawal may be granted by the CLA Dean or GS Dean [1], [3]. * **Application Submission:** You must submit an application for Registration Withdrawal to the Educational Affairs Group along with documents explaining your reasons, such as a medical certificate [3]. * **Deadline for Application:** The application for Registration Withdrawal must be submitted by one month prior to the last day of the classes [3]. * **Effective Date:** Withdrawal will be effective from the date indicated on the notice of acceptance issued by the Educational Affairs Group [2]. * **Consequence of Approval:** If Registration Withdrawal is approved, no grades will be given for any registered courses for that term; instead, a "W" will be recorded for those courses [3].
samples.gold_answer: Before deciding, check whether you are still inside the registration change period: after it ends, course-by-course changes are not accepted, and simply stopping attendance without dropping can result in an F. If you cannot continue because of illness or another unavoidable reason, Registration Withdrawal may be possible, but it withdraws all registered courses, not one course, and requires the stated application deadline/documentation. Approved withdrawn courses receive W and do not count in GPA.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly answers the drop/withdrawal distinction and the consequences after the registration change period.
samples.human_judge_notes: Omits registration-change-period and F-risk framing.

samples.question: I am trying to plan my major requirements and study abroad at the same time. Where should I look to confirm whether courses or credits will count toward my ICU requirements?
samples.answer_text: To confirm whether courses or credits will count toward your ICU requirements, you should look for information regarding transferring credits from other universities or study abroad programs [1]. Specifically: * For summer programs approved by ICU, students who complete coursework at other universities may transfer credits if they are approved to count toward ICU's graduation requirements [1]. A maximum of 40 credits may be transferred, and these transferred credits will count toward Elective Course requirements [1]. * For Study Abroad Programs, credits earned at partner institutions can be recognized as part of the credit unit requirements for graduation from ICU, up to a maximum of 40 credits after a review process [3]. * ICU students can take selected courses at partner universities, such as the University of Tsukuba, and have the earned credits counted toward graduation requirements through a review process [4], [5]. * ICU students can take selected courses at Sophia University and have the earned credits counted toward graduation requirements through a reciprocal credit system [5].
samples.gold_answer: For study-abroad or other transfer credits, check the eHANDBOOK transfer-credit/study-abroad pages and the official study abroad materials. The retrieved evidence says credits count toward ICU graduation only after approval/review, with limits such as 40 credits for study abroad/summer abroad contexts and 60 credits for transfer students. It does not determine whether a specific course will count for your major requirement, so use the official credit-transfer procedure and Educational Affairs Group review for that confirmation.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence supports general transfer-credit review and limits, but not a major-specific decision for a particular course.
retrieved_passage_titles: University/Community Partners of ICU｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY
University/Community Partners of ICU｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY
samples.human_judge_notes: Omits the limitation about specific major requirements.

samples.question: If I take a leave of absence and later return to ICU, what procedure or office information should I check before planning my courses again?
samples.answer_text: Before planning your courses again after taking a leave of absence and returning to ICU, you should check with your faculty members, as they may ask you to provide documentation such as a doctor's certificate or a prescription record book for sick leave or missing classes for reasons other than infectious diseases [3]. You can look up faculty contact information through icuMAP [3].
samples.gold_answer: Before planning courses again, check the eHANDBOOK Leave of Absence and Reenrolment page and Educational Affairs Group information. Leave of absence uses a prescribed form with required signatures and deadlines; from AY2024 Autumn, the re-enrolment procedure itself is generally no longer required, but if the leave was for illness you must submit a physician's certificate to EAG by the stated deadline or you cannot participate in course registration. Non-Japanese citizens who need a student-visa CoE should email EAG at [gakuseki@icu.ac.jp](mailto:gakuseki@icu.ac.jp) early because the CoE process usually takes 1.5–2 months.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly covers leave procedures, reenrolment status, illness documentation, and CoE contact/timing.

samples.question: If I need exam accommodations because of a disability or health condition, where should I start, and what kind of official support page should I look for?
samples.answer_text: If you need reasonable accommodations for a course due to a disability or chronic illness, you should consult with your course instructor and the Learning Accessibility Services [1, 2, 4, 5]. ICU provides reasonable accommodations in accordance with the "Basic Policy for Supporting Students with Disabilities" [1, 5]. For accommodations related to exams and assignments, the Learning Accessibility Services office plays an intermediary role between a student and an instructor to request or arrange necessary accommodations [3].
samples.gold_answer: Start by contacting Learning Accessibility Services (LAS) and consulting your course instructor. The official support page to look for is CTL/LAS For Students or academic accommodations: the process includes meeting LAS staff, submitting medical/specialist documentation and a request form, developing an accommodation plan, and having LAS send requests to professors. Exam-related accommodations may include extended time, a separate room, scribes/typing support, or assistive laptops.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly identifies LAS, documentation steps, instructor consultation, and examples of exam accommodations.
samples.human_judge_notes: Gives the starting point but omits official CTL/process details and over-cites irrelevant sources.

samples.question: I am graduating from undergraduate school but continuing to ICU graduate school. What should I know about my IT account or services after graduation?
samples.answer_text: If you are graduating from your undergraduate school and continuing to ICU graduate school, new accounts will be issued after Matriculation day [4]. Regarding your existing accounts: * Your account (ICU Net ID, ICU Portal, Mail, Google Workspace, etc. ) will expire on March 31, 2026 [1]. * If you still need to exchange emails after graduation, it is recommended that you use an email address you can use after graduation instead of a @icu. ac. jp email address, as email forwarding will stop [1]. * You should back up any necessary files from Gmail and Google Drive before graduation [1]. When your account is deleted, you will not be able to log in to Google (@icu. jp), see the Joining ceremony announcement, or view other information [1].
samples.gold_answer: Your undergraduate ICU Net ID, ICU Portal, Mail, Google Workspace, and related services expire on March 31, 2026, and email forwarding also stops. This applies even if you are continuing from ICU undergraduate school to ICU graduate school, so back up needed Gmail/Drive files and switch necessary communication to another address before graduation. New accounts are issued after Matriculation Day; an optional alumni email provides Gmail and Google Calendar but not Google Drive.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly addresses account expiration, backup needs, applicability to ICU graduate entrants, and new account timing.

samples.question: 日本語プログラムを履修したいのですが、JLPのレベル分けや履修方法について、どこで公式情報を確認できますか？
samples.answer_text: JLPのレベル分けや履修方法に関する公式情報は、ICUのウェブサイトやJLPのウェブサイトで確認できます[2]。 また、JLP科目の履修には、JLP Placement Test（PT）を受け、その結果によって履修できる科目レベルが決まります[1]。 PTの実施時期と有効期間については、毎年2回（3月・8月）実施され、履修希望学期に対応する時期のPTを受験する必要があります[1]。 JLPの履修要件については、入学時の語学力によって変更される場合があり、9月入学生で日本語の知識がない学生は45単位分のJLPを履修しなければならないとされています[5]。 日本語が母国語であるが読み書きの能力に欠ける者は、「日本語特別教育（Special Japanese）」を履修することで日本語履修要件を満たすことができます[5]。 日本語の能力が高い場合はJLPの履修要件の一部が免除されることがあります[5]。 詳細については、ICUのウェブサイトやJLPのウェブサイトを参照してください[2]。
samples.gold_answer: JLPのレベルは、基本的にプレイスメントテストの結果で決まります。初回授業中にも日本語力の追加評価が行われ、その結果で履修レベルが変更される場合があります。公式情報はICUのJLPページ、JLPウェブサイト、該当するPortal告知で確認し、不明点はJLP Office（ERB II-104、[jlp@icu.ac.jp](mailto:jlp@icu.ac.jp)）に問い合わせてください。
samples.teacher_notes: 検索資料は、JLPのレベル決定方法、初回授業での再評価、公式情報源と問い合わせ先を直接示しています。

Notes for graduate school students who wish to take the JLP courses
samples.human_judge_notes: Omits first-class reassessment and JLP Office while adding extensive requirement details.

samples.question: I saw something about Chapel Hour and Christianity Week, but I am not sure whether these are academic events or campus-life events. Where should I check the schedule?
samples.answer_text: * **Chapel Hour:** This is held every Tuesday during the lunch period [2], [4]. It is a time for students, faculty, and staff to pray and reaffirm the foundation of ICU [4]. University ministers, staff members, students, and guests from outside the university speak during this time [2], [4]. It is open to everyone [4]. * **Christianity Week (C-Week):** This is held in May every year [1]. A committee led by students organizes various events related to Christianity for the entire university under a special timetable [1]. These events include early-hour services and open houses where the President and other faculty members who live on campus invite undergraduate and graduate students to their homes [1]. Christianity Week involves a full week of daily programs, including addresses by outstanding speakers and group discussions in faculty homes and elsewhere [2].
samples.gold_answer: These are Religious Center/Christian commitment activities rather than regular courses; the evidence says participation in these activities is optional. For the schedule, check the Religious Center Chapel Hour page and current ICU Portal Chapel Hour notices; for how the events fit into the academic calendar or class timetable, also check the eHANDBOOK class schedule and ICU Academic Calendar. Chapel Hour is shown in current evidence as Tuesdays 1:20–1:50, and C-Week includes special events in May.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly identifies the activity type, optional nature, schedule pages, and current Chapel Hour/C-Week timing.

samples.question: For Spring 2026, I need to know both the registration period and the advisor interview timing. Which official calendar or registration pages should I check?
samples.answer_text: For the AY2026 Spring Term, the following information is provided regarding the registration period and advisor interview timing: * **Online Input:** April 9 (Thu) from 9:00 to April 10 (Fri) at 14:30 [1], [2] * **Advisor Interview:** April 9 (Thu) from 10:00 to 16:00, and April 10 (Fri) from 9:00 to 15:30 [1], [2] For newly enrolled students from the AY2026 Autumn Term, you should check "Campus Pathfinder" in Moodle instead of this announcement [1], [2]. You should check icuMAP in advance to see who your advisor is [1], [2]. You should receive a message from your advisor with details about their advising method (location, time set, etc. ) sometime after April 6 [1]. If you do not hear from your advisor by the evening of April 6, you should send an email to confirm the interview details [1], [2].
samples.gold_answer: For Spring 2026, check the ICU Portal notice “AY2026 Spring Term Registration – Course Registration Procedures” and the Portal calendar event “Registration for Spring Term and Interview with Advisors.” The notice gives online input as April 9, 2026 9:00 to April 10, 2026 14:30; advisor interviews as April 9 10:00–16:00 and April 10 9:00–15:30; and advisor online approval as April 9 10:00 to April 10 16:30. It also lists late registration and registration change from April 11 to April 20, 2026.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly provides the Spring 2026 registration/advisor dates and the Portal notice/calendar sources.

samples.question: I remember there is a class called Statistical Information Literacy. How is the grade evaluated, and what kind of work does the syllabus seem to require?
samples.answer_text: The grading policy for the Statistical Information Literacy course varies depending on the term: * For the Spring 2026 term, the grading is: Exam (20%), Report (40%), Daily quiz (20%), and Class participation (20%) [1]. * For the Autumn 2026 term, the grading includes: Active participation in classes (15%), Application workshops (35%), and an End of term data analysis project (50%) [2]. The required work or content of the course includes: * The course covers topics such as average, variance, conditional probability, independence, random variables, characteristics of distributions, distributions, and inference from sample data (including various interpretations due to differences in measurement periods) [5]. * The course lectures cover the rudiments of statistics, sampling methods, and data processing methods, and students use computers during the course [4].
samples.gold_answer: The grading depends on the section. In Spring 2026, the Japanese section is evaluated by Exam 20%, Report 40%, Daily quiz 20%, and Class participation 20%; in Autumn 2026, the English section is evaluated by Active participation 15%, Application workshops 35%, and an end-of-term data analysis project 50%. The syllabus descriptions suggest work in basic statistics, sampling/data processing, probability, distributions, sample data analysis, and computer/statistical-software-based data analysis.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly gives grading percentages and course-work topics for the relevant sections.
samples.human_judge_notes: Complete grading and workload summary.

samples.question: I need to buy textbooks for Spring 2026 courses. Where should I check the official textbook purchase information, and what details should I not expect the chatbot to know?
samples.answer_text: To purchase textbooks for the Spring Term AY2026, you should check the "Spring AY2026 Textbook Purchase" information [3]. Details regarding the purchase include: * Textbooks will be on sale from Monday, April 6 to Monday, April 20 [3], [4]. * You can complete the purchase using a smartphone or another device via a 2D code or URL provided in the guidance document [3], [4]. * You can pick up your textbooks at an on-campus location for this semester; refer to the guidance document for specific pickup dates, times, and locations [3], [4]. * Textbooks are sold at a 10% discount off the list price (excluding digital textbooks) [3], [4]. * Shipping fees apply for home delivery [3], [4]. You should not expect the chatbot to know the specific details of the guidance document, pickup dates, locations, or the list of textbooks [3], [4].
samples.gold_answer: Check the ICU Portal notice “Spring AY2026 Textbook Purchase” and its purchase guide/list of textbooks. The evidence says sales run from April 6 to April 20, 2026, purchases use the 2D code or URL in the guidance document, pickup dates/times/locations are in that guidance, textbooks are 10% off list price except digital textbooks, and home delivery has shipping fees. The evidence does not define chatbot capabilities, so do not expect a chatbot to replace the current Portal guidance for details such as the purchase URL/2D code, pickup logistics, or the official textbook list; contact the OnSMaRT desk at [smarts@maruzen.co.jp](mailto:smarts@maruzen.co.jp) if needed.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence supports official textbook-purchase information, but not a specific policy on chatbot capabilities or limitations.

samples.question: Can I use ChatGPT for my senior thesis at ICU?
samples.regression_focus: English ChatGPT thesis policy should avoid over-answering from weak evidence

samples.gold_answer: You may not use ChatGPT or other generative AI for a senior thesis in an unauthorized or unspecified way. The Senior Thesis syllabus notes say students must obey ICU Generative AI policies, and that unauthorized/unspecified generative-AI use at any stage of writing the senior thesis, or presenting work produced by others including generative AI as entirely your own, breaches Academic Integrity and receives an E grade. Evidence therefore supports use only when it is authorized/specified and policy-compliant.

samples.answer_text: ICU의 졸업 논문에 ChatGPT를 사용할 수 있는지에 대한 명확한 정보는 제공된 증거에서 찾을 수 없습니다. 다만, 다음과 같은 관련 정책이 있습니다. * 졸업 논문(Senior Thesis)의 경우, 생성형 AI의 무단 및 비특정적 사용, 또는 생성형 AI가 생성한 결과물을 전적으로 본인의 결과물인 것처럼 제시하는 것은 ICU의 학문적 정직성 정책 위반이며 E 학점을 받을 수 있습니다 [2]. * 학생들은 ICU의 생성형 AI 사용에 관한 정책을 준수해야 합니다 [2]. * 일반적으로 수업은 ICU의 생성형 AI 사용에 관한 정책을 따르며, 예외 사항은 수업 시간에 논의될 수 있습니다 [3].
samples.gold_answer: 检索到的毕业论文课程资料足以回答：不能以未经授权或未明确说明的方式在毕业论文写作的任何阶段使用ChatGPT等生成式AI。资料还说，必须遵守ICU的生成式AI政策；把他人或生成式AI生成的内容当作完全由自己完成的作品提交，属于违反学术诚信，并会得到E等级。换言之，只有在被明确允许并符合ICU政策的情况下才可使用。
samples.teacher_notes: 证据直接给出毕业论文课程的生成式AI规则，并由ICU本科生生成式AI方针补充支持。

samples.human_judge_notes: Answers in Korean to a Chinese question, though the policy content is mostly correct.

samples.question: After I select courses on the registration website, how can I check whether my registration is actually complete?
samples.regression_focus: English version of registration completion procedure
samples.answer_text: After selecting courses on the registration website, you must check the registration results on the "Registration Result" section of the Registration Website to confirm that your registration is complete and that you have received online approval from your advisor [1, 2, 5]. You should also check the results after making any changes to your registration [1, 3].
samples.gold_answer: After selecting courses, check the Registration Website Registration Result(s) page. Course registration is not complete until you have the advisor interview and your advisor approves the registration online, so confirm both the registered courses and advisor approval on the next day of Registration Day or after Late Registration; also recheck after any add/drop. The evidence says registration confirmation is the student's responsibility and no registration/change is accepted after the specified period.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly states how to confirm registration completion, the advisor-approval requirement, and the student's responsibility to check results.
samples.human_judge_notes: Covers Registration Result, advisor approval, and rechecking after changes.

samples.question: I missed the normal course registration day because I was sick. Can I still register or change my courses, and what part of the procedure should I pay attention to?
samples.answer_text: If you missed the normal registration day, you should complete your registration during the registration change period [2], [3], [4]. After the registration change period ends, the registration will not be accepted for any reason [2], [4]. If you do not complete the registration during the designated period, you must take a leave of absence or withdraw from the university [2], [3], [5]. When making a registration change, be sure to click the "SAVE" button to complete the procedure, as the process is not finished unless this button is clicked [2], [3], [4], [5]. After the registration and registration change, confirm that the courses you want to take are correctly reflected [3], [4], [5].
samples.gold_answer: If you missed Registration Day, you should complete Late Registration during the registration change period; after that period, no registration is accepted for any reason. Complete the procedure with the SAVE button, confirm your added/cancelled courses in Registration Results, and receive your advisor's interview/online approval. Late Registration requires the 3,000 yen fee before advisor approval.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly states the late-registration window, no-registration-after-period rule, SAVE/results checks, advisor approval, and fee.

samples.question: If I want to withdraw from a course after seeing my workload, what rules or deadlines should I check before I make the decision?
samples.answer_text: To withdraw from a course, you should check the following: * **Withdrawal Type:** Registration Withdrawal means withdrawing all course registration for an ongoing term, and it is not possible to withdraw course registration partly [1], [3]. * **Application Submission Deadline:** * For Withdrawal from the Spring Term: March 31 [2] * For Withdrawal from the Autumn Term: August 31 [2] * For Withdrawal from the Winter Term: November 30 [2] * The deadline is the previous Friday if the stipulated date falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or national holiday [2]. * **Application Process:** You should submit a completed form to the Educational Affairs Group along with your ID Card [2]. If you are withdrawing due to illness or an unavoidable reason recognized by the University, permission for Registration Withdrawal may be given by the CLA Dean or GS Dean [1], [3]. * **Timing:** Withdrawal will be effective from the date indicated on the notice of acceptance issued by the Educational Affairs Group [2].
samples.gold_answer: Before deciding, check whether you are still inside the registration change period: after it ends, course-by-course changes are not accepted, and simply stopping attendance without dropping can result in an F. If you cannot continue because of illness or another unavoidable reason, Registration Withdrawal may be possible, but it withdraws all registered courses, not one course, and requires the stated application deadline/documentation. Approved withdrawn courses receive W and do not count in GPA.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly answers the drop/withdrawal distinction and the consequences after the registration change period.
retrieved_passage_titles: ehandbook - August 28, 2024 Important Information from Educational Affairs Group
samples.human_judge_notes: Misses registration-change, F-risk, and W/GPA points while mixing in withdrawal deadlines.

samples.question: I am trying to plan my major requirements and study abroad at the same time. Where should I look to confirm whether courses or credits will count toward my ICU requirements?
samples.answer_text: * **Summer Abroad Programs:** Students who participate in summer programs approved by ICU and complete the coursework at other universities may transfer the credits if they are approved to count toward ICU's graduation requirements [1]. * **Partner Institutions:** You can take selected courses at partner universities and have the credits you earn counted toward graduation requirements at ICU through a review process [3], [4]. For example, ICU students can take selected courses at the University of Tsukuba and have the credits earned counted toward graduation requirements at ICU through the review process [3], [4]. Similarly, ICU students can take selected courses at Sophia University and have the credits earned counted toward graduation requirements at ICU through the review process [4]. * **Transfer Student Credits:** Students who have completed courses at other universities before entering ICU may transfer those credits if the courses are considered equivalent to courses offered at ICU, are approved as applicable toward ICU graduation requirements, and if the grades received were 70 points or higher out of 100 [5]. Credit transfers will only be granted after you complete the required procedures after entering ICU, and the exact number and which credits are transferable will not be confirmed until then [5].
samples.gold_answer: For study-abroad or other transfer credits, check the eHANDBOOK transfer-credit/study-abroad pages and the official study abroad materials. The retrieved evidence says credits count toward ICU graduation only after approval/review, with limits such as 40 credits for study abroad/summer abroad contexts and 60 credits for transfer students. It does not determine whether a specific course will count for your major requirement, so use the official credit-transfer procedure and Educational Affairs Group review for that confirmation.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence supports general transfer-credit review and limits, but not a major-specific decision for a particular course.

samples.question: If I take a leave of absence and later return to ICU, what procedure or office information should I check before planning my courses again?
samples.answer_text: If you take a leave of absence due to a medical condition and plan to return, you must submit a physician's certificate attesting that the condition will not affect your studies at ICU after your return to the Educational Affairs Group (EAG) [1]. Failure to submit this form may result in you being unable to participate in course registration [1].
samples.gold_answer: Before planning courses again, check the eHANDBOOK Leave of Absence and Reenrolment page and Educational Affairs Group information. Leave of absence uses a prescribed form with required signatures and deadlines; from AY2024 Autumn, the re-enrolment procedure itself is generally no longer required, but if the leave was for illness you must submit a physician's certificate to EAG by the stated deadline or you cannot participate in course registration. Non-Japanese citizens who need a student-visa CoE should email EAG at [gakuseki@icu.ac.jp](mailto:gakuseki@icu.ac.jp) early because the CoE process usually takes 1.5–2 months.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly covers leave procedures, reenrolment status, illness documentation, and CoE contact/timing.

samples.question: If I need exam accommodations because of a disability or health condition, where should I start, and what kind of official support page should I look for?
samples.answer_text: If you need exam accommodations due to a disability or health condition, you should first meet with a Learning Accessibility Services (LAS) staff member to discuss your needs [1]. The LAS office acts as an intermediary between you and an instructor to request or arrange necessary accommodations for exams and assignments [2], [3]. To apply for academic accommodations, the steps include: 1. Meeting with a LAS staff member to discuss your needs [1]. 2. Submitting necessary documents, such as a medical certificate from your doctor or a letter from a counselor or other specialist, and a Request for Academic Accommodations Form (which must be signed by your advisor) [1]. 3. Developing an accommodation plan with a member of the LAS staff [1]. 4. The LAS office delivers your accommodation requests to your professors [1]. For more information specific to your needs, you can contact the LAS office [2].
samples.gold_answer: Start by contacting Learning Accessibility Services (LAS) and consulting your course instructor. The official support page to look for is CTL/LAS For Students or academic accommodations: the process includes meeting LAS staff, submitting medical/specialist documentation and a request form, developing an accommodation plan, and having LAS send requests to professors. Exam-related accommodations may include extended time, a separate room, scribes/typing support, or assistive laptops.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly identifies LAS, documentation steps, instructor consultation, and examples of exam accommodations.
samples.human_judge_notes: Covers the process and LAS contact.

samples.question: I am graduating from undergraduate school but continuing to ICU graduate school. What should I know about my IT account or services after graduation?
samples.answer_text: If you are graduating from your undergraduate school and continuing to ICU graduate school, new accounts will be issued after Matriculation day [4]. For accounts such as ICU Net ID, ICU Portal, Mail, and Google Workspace, these accounts will expire on March 31, 2026 [1]. If you still need to exchange emails after graduation, it is recommended that you use an email address you can use after graduation, rather than a @icu.ac.jp email address, as email forwarding will stop [1]. You should back up any necessary files from Gmail and Google Drive before graduation [1]. When your account is deleted, you will not be able to log in to Google (@icu.ac.jp), you will not see the announcement of the Joining ceremony, and you will not see information about graduate school files created while you were at ICU [1].
samples.gold_answer: Your undergraduate ICU Net ID, ICU Portal, Mail, Google Workspace, and related services expire on March 31, 2026, and email forwarding also stops. This applies even if you are continuing from ICU undergraduate school to ICU graduate school, so back up needed Gmail/Drive files and switch necessary communication to another address before graduation. New accounts are issued after Matriculation Day; an optional alumni email provides Gmail and Google Calendar but not Google Drive.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly addresses account expiration, backup needs, applicability to ICU graduate entrants, and new account timing.

samples.question: 日本語プログラムを履修したいのですが、JLPのレベル分けや履修方法について、どこで公式情報を確認できますか？
samples.answer_text: JLPのレベル分けは、プレースメントテストの結果に基づいて決定されます[2]。入学後にプレースメントテストを受け、その結果により最高で45単位のJLP科目が課されますが、日本語能力が十分にあると認められる者はJLPが免除される場合があります[1]。 JLPには、日本語の学習目標としてCEFRレベルが用いられ、CEFRはA1からC2までの6つのレベルで、これらはBasic User、Independent User、Proficient Userの3つの広範なレベルに分類されます[3]。具体的には、「Japanese Track」と「Special Japanese Track」があり、それぞれ異なるCEFRレベルが設定されています[2]。 公式情報を確認するには、JLPのウェブサイトを参照してください[3]。また、JLPコースを受講するには、JLPプレースメントテストを受け、割り当てられたコースに登録し、学術アドバイザーの承認を得る必要があります[4]。
samples.gold_answer: JLPのレベルは、基本的にプレイスメントテストの結果で決まります。初回授業中にも日本語力の追加評価が行われ、その結果で履修レベルが変更される場合があります。公式情報はICUのJLPページ、JLPウェブサイト、該当するPortal告知で確認し、不明点はJLP Office（ERB II-104、[jlp@icu.ac.jp](mailto:jlp@icu.ac.jp)）に問い合わせてください。
samples.teacher_notes: 検索資料は、JLPのレベル決定方法、初回授業での再評価、公式情報源と問い合わせ先を直接示しています。
retrieved_passage_titles: 自己点検・評価報告書（2009年）
Notes for graduate school students who wish to take the JLP courses

samples.question: I transferred credits before entering ICU. Can the system tell me exactly how many more credits I personally need to graduate early?
samples.answer_text: If you transferred credits before entering ICU, you may be able to transfer those credits if the courses are considered equivalent to those offered at ICU or are approved to be counted towards graduation requirements [4]. The grades for these courses must be C (70/100) or higher [4]. The maximum number of credits that can be transferred depends on your situation: * Students who spent more than 1 year at other universities or have obtained an associate degree may transfer up to 60 credits [4]. * Within the maximum number of credits, up to 12 credits of General Education Courses, 1 credit of Health and Physical Education Lecture Courses, 1 credit of Physical Education Exercise Courses, 9 credits of Foundation Courses, and 41 credits of Elective Courses may be transferred [4]. The evidence does not provide a mechanism to calculate the exact remaining credits you need for early graduation [1, 2, 3, 4, 5].
samples.gold_answer: The retrieved evidence cannot calculate your personal remaining credits or confirm early graduation. It only says ICU students need at least 130 credits, credit requirements vary by language proficiency and other conditions, and transfer credits are confirmed only after the required procedures after entering ICU; how many and which credits are transferable are not confirmed until then. Use your official credit-transfer result and graduation requirements record for the exact number.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence supports general credit-transfer and graduation-credit rules, but not a personalized remaining-credit calculation.

samples.question: 履修登録について教育事務グループに確認したいです。連絡先や関連する公式ページはどこで確認できますか？
samples.answer_text: 履修登録について、以下の点を確認してください。 * 一部の科目の履修登録には、申請書を教育事務グループに提出する必要があります [1]。 * 申請書は、登録日の1週間前から教育事務グループ窓口で配布されます [1]。 * 申請の提出期間は、登録日から登録変更期間最終日の10:00までです [1]。 * 制限受講単位数17単位を超える履修を希望する場合、累積GPAが3.40以上で、指導教員と学部長の許可を得る必要があります [1]。この場合、申請書には指導教員の署名が必要です [1]。 詳細については、ehandbookの「履修登録」の項目を参照してください [5]。
samples.gold_answer: 履修登録・成績・卒業要件に関する問い合わせ先として、教務グループ（Educational Affairs Group）[ea-group@icu.ac.jp](mailto:ea-group@icu.ac.jp) が示されています。関連する公式ページは、eHANDBOOKの「履修登録」ページと、各学期のICU Portal/eHANDBOOKに出る教務グループからの科目登録に関する重要なお知らせです。履修登録ページでは、Registration Websiteでの入力、アドヴァイザー面談・オンライン承認、Registration Resultsの確認が説明されています。
samples.teacher_notes: 検索資料は、教務グループの問い合わせ先と履修登録関連の公式ページ・確認事項を直接示しています。

samples.question: I remember there is a class called Statistical Information Literacy. How is the grade evaluated, and what kind of work does the syllabus seem to require?
samples.answer_text: The grading policy for S2: Statistical Information Literacy varies by term: * For the Spring 2026 term, the grading is: Exam (20%), Report (40%), Daily quiz (20%), and Class participation (20%) [1]. * For the Autumn 2026 term, the grading is: Active participation in classes (15%), Application workshops (35%), and End of term data analysis project (50%) [2]. The course content involves learning the basic skill of data analysis for studying the social sciences, including lectures on the rudiments of statistics, sampling methods, and data processing methods. Students will use computers during the course [4]. The course will also cover the mathematical foundations of probability, randomness, and how this informs the understanding of how real-world data may be generated [4].
samples.gold_answer: The grading depends on the section. In Spring 2026, the Japanese section is evaluated by Exam 20%, Report 40%, Daily quiz 20%, and Class participation 20%; in Autumn 2026, the English section is evaluated by Active participation 15%, Application workshops 35%, and an end-of-term data analysis project 50%. The syllabus descriptions suggest work in basic statistics, sampling/data processing, probability, distributions, sample data analysis, and computer/statistical-software-based data analysis.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly gives grading percentages and course-work topics for the relevant sections.
samples.human_judge_notes: Complete grading and workload summary.

samples.question: MIURA先生って、どの日本語の授業を担当していますか？レベルも少し知りたいです。
samples.regression_focus: teacher entity and JLP course retrieval
samples.answer_text: MIURA先生は、「J2: Japanese」という日本語の授業を担当しています [1], [2], [3], [4]。この授業は、日本語の話しことばと書きことばについて、CEFR A2.1レベルを目標としており、他者との関わりにおける言語活動に焦点を当てています [1], [2], [4]。また、「日本語J1」または同等のレベルを既修であることが前提条件です [1], [2], [4]。 さらに、MIURA先生は「Vocabulary and Kanji for Intermediate Learners of Japanese 2」という授業でも担当しており、この授業では講義、読解資料、試験、ディスカッションなど、すべて日本語が使用されます [5]。
samples.gold_answer: 検索資料で確認できる日本語科目では、三浦綾乃先生が「J2: Japanese（日本語J2）」を担当しています。J2は日本語の話しことば・書きことばのCEFR A2.1レベルを目標とし、「J1: Japanese」または同等レベルの既修が前提です。ほかに三浦綾乃先生が関わる科目として「Special Japanese Kanji 1」と「Vocabulary and Kanji for Intermediate Learners of Japanese 2」も出ていますが、後者の詳しいレベル説明は検索資料では科目名・教授言語程度に限られます。
samples.teacher_notes: 検索資料は三浦綾乃先生が担当する日本語関連科目と、J2のレベル・前提条件を直接示しています。

samples.question: 履修登録サイトで科目を選択したのに登録が完了したか不安です。どの操作や確認をすればよいですか？
samples.answer_text: 履修登録が完了したか不安な場合は、以下の点を確認してください。 * **登録結果の確認:** 履修登録の翌日、またはLate Registration後は、Registration Websiteの「Registration Result」で登録結果と、アドヴァイザーによるオンライン承認を受けていることを必ず確認してください[1], [2], [3]。登録変更を行った後も、登録結果を必ず確認する必要があります[1], [2], [3]。 * **アドヴァイザーの承認:** 履修登録は、アドヴァイザーからオンラインによる承認を受けて初めて完了します[4]。アドヴァイザーにオンライン承認されるまで、登録手続きは完了していません[4]。 履修登録および登録の確認は学生本人の責任であり、定められた期間を過ぎて履修登録または登録変更を申請しても一切受理されません[1], [3]。
samples.gold_answer: 科目を選択しただけでは完了とは限りません。登録当日にアドヴァイザー面談を受け、アドヴァイザーのオンライン承認を受けて初めて履修登録が完了します。登録後・Late Registration後・登録変更後は、Registration Websiteの「Registration Result(s)」で登録結果とアドヴァイザー承認を必ず確認し、SAVEボタンの押し忘れにも注意してください。
samples.teacher_notes: 検索資料は、履修登録完了条件、登録結果の確認、SAVEボタンへの注意を直接説明しています。
samples.human_judge_notes: Covers Registration Result and advisor approval.

samples.question: I need to buy textbooks for Spring 2026 courses. Where should I check the official textbook purchase information, and what details should I not expect the chatbot to know?
samples.answer_text: To purchase textbooks for the Spring Term AY2026, you should check the provided guidance document for details on how to complete the purchase using a smartphone or other device via the 2D code or URL [1], [2]. Details you should not expect the chatbot to know include: * Textbooks will be on sale from Monday, April 6 to Monday, April 20 [1], [2]. * You can pick up textbooks at an on-campus location, and specific pickup dates, times, and locations can be found in the guidance document [1], [2]. * Textbooks are sold at a 10% discount off the list price (excluding digital textbooks) [1], [2]. * Shipping fees apply for home delivery [1], [2]. * Contact information for inquiries is available through the OnSMaRT desk (MARUZEN-YUSHODO Co., Ltd.) at smarts@maruzen.co.jp [1], [2].
samples.gold_answer: Check the ICU Portal notice “Spring AY2026 Textbook Purchase” and its purchase guide/list of textbooks. The evidence says sales run from April 6 to April 20, 2026, purchases use the 2D code or URL in the guidance document, pickup dates/times/locations are in that guidance, textbooks are 10% off list price except digital textbooks, and home delivery has shipping fees. The evidence does not define chatbot capabilities, so do not expect a chatbot to replace the current Portal guidance for details such as the purchase URL/2D code, pickup logistics, or the official textbook list; contact the OnSMaRT desk at [smarts@maruzen.co.jp](mailto:smarts@maruzen.co.jp) if needed.

It also says your required language program is ELA or JLP depending on admissions route, and that language/specialized credit requirements vary, so check the official Graduation Requirements/eHANDBOOK for your own case.

samples.question: ICUに入学した後、必ず履修しなければならない科目は何ですか？どの公式ページを見るべきですか？
samples.answer_text: ICUでは、卒業のための必修要件として、「健康科学（Health Science）」（1科目・1単位）と実技科目である「PEエクササイズⅠ・Ⅱ・Ⅲ (Physical Education Exercise Ⅰ・Ⅱ・Ⅲ)」（3種目・1単位、各種目は1/3単位）を履修する必要があります[1]。 これらの科目は、大学生活を送る中での「健康」や「安全」について学ぶだけでなく、生涯にわたるQuality of Life（生活の質）の向上につながる知識や技術を学ぶこと、また、実技科目では集団活動におけるコミュニケーション力やリーダーシップ性などを体験を通して身につけることを目指します[1]。 履修に関する詳細は、保健体育のページを確認することが推奨されます[1]。
samples.gold_answer: 検索資料で確認できる必修要件は、保健体育の「健康科学（Health Science）」1科目1単位と「PEエクササイズI・II・III」3種目合計1単位、および入試区分等により決まる必修語学（ELAまたはJLP）です。一方で、入学後に必ず履修する全科目の完全な一覧はこの証拠だけでは断定できません。公式ページは「卒業要件」「保健体育」「ICUの入試について／日英バイリンガル教育」や在学生向けeHANDBOOKを確認してください。
samples.teacher_notes: 検索資料は保健体育と語学の要件を示していますが、必修科目全体の完全な一覧は示していません。

samples.question: 进入ICU以后必须修哪些课程？我应该看哪个官方页面？
samples.answer_text: ICUの卒業要件として、学生は以下の科目を履修する必要があります[1], [2]： * **健康科学 (Health Science)**：1科目・1単位 [1], [2] * **PEエクササイズⅠ・Ⅱ・Ⅲ (Physical Education Exercise Ⅰ・Ⅱ・Ⅲ)**：3種目・1単位（各種目は1/3単位）[1], [2] これらの科目を通じて、学生は健康や安全について学び、生涯にわたる生活の質の向上につながる知識や技術を得ます[1], [2]。また、実技科目では、集団活動におけるコミュニケーション力、リーダーシップ性、フォロアーシップ性などを体験を通して身につけることができます[1], [2]。 これらの必修要件に関する公式ページは、ICUのウェブサイトの「Physical Education」または「保健体育」のページで確認できます[1], [2]。

samples.question: 大学の卒論にチャットGPTを使っても大丈夫ですか？
samples.regression_focus: ChatGPT thesis policy should prefer official policy over newsletter-like matches
samples.answer_text: 大学の卒論にチャットGPTを使用することについて、国際基督教大学（ICU）の学問的インテグリティの方針に基づき、以下の点が重要です。 * **生成系AIの使用について:** ICUは生成系AIの使用を全面的に否定するものではありません [1], [2]。 * **成績評価のための提出物について:** 生成系AIが作成したものを、本学における成績評価のための提出物に使用し、あたかも自分自身が作成したかのように振る舞うことは、アカデミック・インテグリティの方針が定義する「剽窃」に相当します [1], [2]。 * **剽窃の定義:** アカデミック・インテグリティの方針では、「他人の研究に使われている考え方や言葉、文章や調査研究をあたかも自分自身のものであるかのように偽ることは、学問的倫理基準を侵すことになります」とされています [1], [2]。 * **提出物の要件:** 教員の評価を受けるための学生の提出物（レポート、試験の答案など）は、自分自身のもの（オリジナル作品）でなければなりません [1]。学生は自分自身で考え、調査研究したものでない情報の出典は、明らかにし、正しく引用することが求められます [1], [2]。 * **AIの懸念点:** 生成系AIが使用しているデータが他人の著作物などから成り立っているため、AIが出力する回答自体が著作権侵害や剽窃である可能性があるという懸念もあります [1], [2]。 要するに、チャットGPTなどの生成系AIを利用することは可能ですが、その成果物を成績評価のための提出物として提出する際には、**自分自身のオリジナル作品として偽らない**ことが求められます [1], [2]。
samples.gold_answer: 検索資料だけでは、卒論でChatGPTを使ってよいかを卒論専用ルールとして断定することはできません。ただしICUの学部方針では、生成系AIの使用を全面的に否定してはいない一方、生成系AIが作成したものを成績評価のための提出物に自分の作品のように使うことは剽窃にあたるとされています。自分で考え・調査したものではない情報は出典を明示して正しく引用し、AIの出力は不正確性・剽窃・プライバシー等のリスクを批判的に確認する必要があります。
samples.teacher_notes: 検索資料はICUの一般的な生成AI方針を示していますが、卒論専用の許可・禁止条件は示していません。
retrieved_passage_titles: 学部 教育方針｜国際基督教大学（ICU）
教育の特徴：大学院｜国際基督教大学（ICU）
レポート・卒論に役立つコンテンツ | CTL
samples.human_judge_notes: Covers the key ICU academic-integrity limits for generative AI use.

samples.question: Can I use ChatGPT for my senior thesis at ICU?
samples.regression_focus: English ChatGPT thesis policy should avoid over-answering from weak evidence
samples.answer_text: Students must abide by the ICU Generative AI policies when writing their senior thesis [1], [2], [3]. The unauthorized and unspecified use of generative AI in any stage of writing the senior thesis, and presenting work produced by others, including generative AI, as entirely your own work is considered a breach of ICU policy on academic integrity and may result in an E grade [1], [2], [3].
samples.gold_answer: You may not use ChatGPT or other generative AI for a senior thesis in an unauthorized or unspecified way. The Senior Thesis syllabus notes say students must obey ICU Generative AI policies, and that unauthorized/unspecified generative-AI use at any stage of writing the senior thesis, or presenting work produced by others including generative AI as entirely your own, breaches Academic Integrity and receives an E grade. Evidence therefore supports use only when it is authorized/specified and policy-compliant.
samples.teacher_notes: The Senior Thesis syllabus evidence directly states the generative-AI rule and consequence.
samples.human_judge_notes: States the unauthorized/unspecified generative-AI rule and E-grade consequence.

samples.question: 我可以在ICU的毕业论文里使用ChatGPT吗？如果资料不够，请明确告诉我。
samples.regression_focus: Chinese ChatGPT thesis policy and safe refusal
samples.answer_text: 在ICU的毕业论文中，学生必须遵守ICU的生成式人工智能政策。未经授权和未指明的生成式人工智能在撰写毕业论文的任何阶段的使用，以及将由生成式人工智能产生的作品（包括生成式人工智能的作品）作为完全由自己完成的作品提交，构成违反ICU学术诚信政策，并可能导致E等级 [1]。 ICU强调批判性思维，要求学生以自己的话思考、研究、反思并组织他们的回答 [3]。生成式人工智能生成的文本，即使经过一些修改，也不被视为自己的作品，并可能被视为违反学术诚信政策 [3]。对于需要自己完成的任务，不应使用生成式人工智能 [4, 5]。在使用生成式人工智能时，需要进行适当的审慎，细致的关注和专业知识，并且有必要批判性地审查输出并更正任何问题 [3]。依赖生成式人工智能可能意味着放弃人类创作词语和句子的努力，阻碍在创造力、批判性思维以及信息收集、组织、讨论、写作和语言学习等方面的成长 [3]。
samples.gold_answer: 检索到的毕业论文课程资料足以回答：不能以未经授权或未明确说明的方式在毕业论文写作的任何阶段使用ChatGPT等生成式AI。资料还说，必须遵守ICU的生成式AI政策；把他人或生成式AI生成的内容当作完全由自己完成的作品提交，属于违反学术诚信，并会得到E等级。换言之，只有在被明确允许并符合ICU政策的情况下才可使用。
samples.teacher_notes: 证据直接给出毕业论文课程的生成式AI规则，并由ICU本科生生成式AI方针补充支持。

samples.question: I missed the normal course registration day because I was sick. Can I still register or change my courses, and what part of the procedure should I pay attention to?
samples.answer_text: If you miss the normal registration day, you should complete registration during the registration change period [3, 4]. After the registration change period ends, registration will not be accepted for any reason, and you must take a leave of absence or withdraw from the university [3, 4]. When making a registration change, you must click the "SAVE" button to finalize the procedure; otherwise, the process is not complete even if you input the data [3, 4]. Additionally, after registration or registration change, you should confirm that the courses you wish to add or cancel are correctly reflected on the Registration Website "Registration Results" [5].
samples.gold_answer: If you missed Registration Day, you should complete Late Registration during the registration change period; after that period, no registration is accepted for any reason. Complete the procedure with the SAVE button, confirm your added/cancelled courses in Registration Results, and receive your advisor's interview/online approval. Late Registration requires the 3,000 yen fee before advisor approval.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly states the late-registration window, no-registration-after-period rule, SAVE/results checks, advisor approval, and fee.

samples.human_judge_notes: Omits advisor approval and the fee.

samples.question: If I want to withdraw from a course after seeing my workload, what rules or deadlines should I check before I make the decision?
samples.answer_text: If you cannot continue your studies for an ongoing term due to an illness or an unavoidable reason recognized by the University, you may be able to receive permission for Registration Withdrawal from the CLA Dean or GS Dean [1, 3]. Key points regarding Registration Withdrawal: * Registration Withdrawal means withdrawing from **all** course registrations for the ongoing term; partial withdrawal is not possible [3]. * An application for Registration Withdrawal must be submitted to the Educational Affairs Group along with documents explaining the reasonable reasons, such as a medical certificate [3]. * The application must be submitted one month prior to the last day of classes [3]. * If approved, no grades will be given for the registered courses; instead, "W" will be recorded for all courses registered for that term [3].
samples.gold_answer: Before deciding, check whether you are still inside the registration change period: after it ends, course-by-course changes are not accepted, and simply stopping attendance without dropping can result in an F. If you cannot continue because of illness or another unavoidable reason, Registration Withdrawal may be possible, but it withdraws all registered courses, not one course, and requires the stated application deadline/documentation. Approved withdrawn courses receive W and do not count in GPA.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly answers the drop/withdrawal distinction and the consequences after the registration change period.
samples.human_judge_notes: Omits registration-change and F-risk framing.

samples.question: I am trying to plan my major requirements and study abroad at the same time. Where should I look to confirm whether courses or credits will count toward my ICU requirements?
samples.answer_text: To confirm if courses or credits from study abroad will count toward your ICU requirements, you should look at the guidelines regarding the transfer of credits from other universities [1]. Specifically: * Students who participated in ICU-approved summer programs and completed coursework at other universities may transfer credits if they are approved to count toward ICU's graduation requirements [1]. * A maximum of 40 credits may be transferred from study abroad programs, and these transferred credits will count toward Elective Course requirements [1]. * For students who participated in a Study Abroad Program, credits earned at partner institutions, up to a maximum of 40 credits, are recognized as part of the credit unit requirements for graduation from ICU [3]. * ICU students can take selected courses at Sophia University and have the credits earned counted toward graduation requirements through a reciprocal credit system [4, 5].
samples.gold_answer: For study-abroad or other transfer credits, check the eHANDBOOK transfer-credit/study-abroad pages and the official study abroad materials. The retrieved evidence says credits count toward ICU graduation only after approval/review, with limits such as 40 credits for study abroad/summer abroad contexts and 60 credits for transfer students. It does not determine whether a specific course will count for your major requirement, so use the official credit-transfer procedure and Educational Affairs Group review for that confirmation.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence supports general transfer-credit review and limits, but not a major-specific decision for a particular course.

samples.question: If I take a leave of absence and later return to ICU, what procedure or office information should I check before planning my courses again?
samples.answer_text: The provided ICU evidence does not specify the procedure or office information you should check before planning your courses after returning from a leave of absence. samples.gold_answer: Before planning courses again, check the eHANDBOOK Leave of Absence and Reenrolment page and Educational Affairs Group information. Leave of absence uses a prescribed form with required signatures and deadlines; from AY2024 Autumn, the re-enrolment procedure itself is generally no longer required, but if the leave was for illness you must submit a physician's certificate to EAG by the stated deadline or you cannot participate in course registration. Non-Japanese citizens who need a student-visa CoE should email EAG at [gakuseki@icu.ac.jp](mailto:gakuseki@icu.ac.jp) early because the CoE process usually takes 1.5–2 months.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly covers leave procedures, reenrolment status, illness documentation, and CoE contact/timing.

samples.question: If I need exam accommodations because of a disability or health condition, where should I start, and what kind of official support page should I look for?
samples.answer_text: If you have a disability or chronic illness and require reasonable accommodations for a course, you should consult with your course instructor and the Learning Accessibility Services [1, 2, 4, 5]. The Learning Accessibility Services office acts as an intermediary between a student and an instructor to arrange necessary accommodations for exams and assignments [3].
samples.gold_answer: Start by contacting Learning Accessibility Services (LAS) and consulting your course instructor. The official support page to look for is CTL/LAS For Students or academic accommodations: the process includes meeting LAS staff, submitting medical/specialist documentation and a request form, developing an accommodation plan, and having LAS send requests to professors. Exam-related accommodations may include extended time, a separate room, scribes/typing support, or assistive laptops.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly identifies LAS, documentation steps, instructor consultation, and examples of exam accommodations.

samples.question: I am graduating from undergraduate school but continuing to ICU graduate school. What should I know about my IT account or services after graduation?
samples.answer_text: If you are an undergraduate student who is continuing to ICU graduate school, new accounts will be issued after Matriculation day [4]. For students graduating, your account (including ICU Net ID, ICU Portal, Mail, and Google Workspace) will expire on March 31, 2026 [1]. If you still need to access emails after graduation, it is recommended that you use an email address you can use after graduation, rather than a @icu. ac. jp address, because email forwarding will stop [1]. You should also back up any necessary files from Gmail and Google Drive before graduation [1].
samples.gold_answer: Your undergraduate ICU Net ID, ICU Portal, Mail, Google Workspace, and related services expire on March 31, 2026, and email forwarding also stops. This applies even if you are continuing from ICU undergraduate school to ICU graduate school, so back up needed Gmail/Drive files and switch necessary communication to another address before graduation. New accounts are issued after Matriculation Day; an optional alumni email provides Gmail and Google Calendar but not Google Drive.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly addresses account expiration, backup needs, applicability to ICU graduate entrants, and new account timing.

samples.question: 履修登録サイトで科目を選択したのに登録が完了したか不安です。どの操作や確認をすればよいですか？
samples.answer_text: 科目を選択した後、登録が完了したか不安な場合は、以下の操作と確認を行ってください。 * **登録結果の確認:** 登録または登録変更を行った後は、必ずRegistration Websiteの「Registration Results」で登録結果を確認してください [4]。 * **アドバイザーの承認確認:** アドバイザーにオンライン承認されるまで、登録手続きは完了していません [1], [2], [3]。 また、登録や変更を行った後は、アドバイザー承認が下りているか自身で確認してください [4]。 なお、登録確認は学生本人の責任であり、定められた期間を過ぎて履修登録または登録変更を申請しても一切受理されません [1], [2]。
samples.gold_answer: 科目を選択しただけでは完了とは限りません。登録当日にアドヴァイザー面談を受け、アドヴァイザーのオンライン承認を受けて初めて履修登録が完了します。登録後・Late Registration後・登録変更後は、Registration Websiteの「Registration Result(s)」で登録結果とアドヴァイザー承認を必ず確認し、SAVEボタンの押し忘れにも注意してください。
samples.teacher_notes: 検索資料は、履修登録完了条件、登録結果の確認、SAVEボタンへの注意を直接説明しています。

samples.question: ICUに入学した後、必ず履修しなければならない科目は何ですか？どの公式ページを見るべきですか？
samples.answer_text: ICUに入学後に必ず履修しなければならない科目は、専攻や入学した選抜によって異なります。 * **保健体育**の分野では、卒業のための必修要件として「健康科学（Health Science）」（1科目・1単位）と実技科目である「PEエクササイズⅠ・Ⅱ・Ⅲ (Physical Education Exercise Ⅰ・Ⅱ・Ⅲ)」（3種目・1単位、各種目は1/3単位）を履修します [1]。 * 教員免許状の取得を志望する学生は、教職課程の必修科目として「体育理論（Physical Education）」を履修します [1]。 * 卒業要件上の必修語学科目は、受験する選抜によって異なり、一般選抜や総合型選抜<4月入学専願>の場合は「リベラルアーツ英語プログラム」（ELA: English for Liberal Arts Program）が必修となります [5]。 総合型選抜<4月/9月入学>English Language Based Admissionsの場合は「日本語教育プログラム」（JLP: Japanese Language Program）が必修となります [5]。 どの公式ページを見るべきかについては、具体的な情報として、**「学生募集要項」**や**「アドミッションズ・センターのホームページ」**を確認することが推奨されています [2, 5]。

samples.question: Can I use ChatGPT for my senior thesis at ICU?
samples.regression_focus: English ChatGPT thesis policy should avoid over-answering from weak evidence
samples.answer_text: The retrieved ICU evidence is not sufficient to answer this as a clear yes/no policy judgment. The closest retrieved sources are [1] Senior Thesis and [3] PDF. Please check the official ICU policy page or ask the relevant office before relying on this.
samples.gold_answer: You may not use ChatGPT or other generative AI for a senior thesis in an unauthorized or unspecified way. The Senior Thesis syllabus notes say students must obey ICU Generative AI policies, and that unauthorized/unspecified generative-AI use at any stage of writing the senior thesis, or presenting work produced by others including generative AI as entirely your own, breaches Academic Integrity and receives an E grade. Evidence therefore supports use only when it is authorized/specified and policy-compliant.
samples.teacher_notes: The Senior Thesis syllabus evidence directly states the generative-AI rule and consequence.

samples.question: I missed the normal course registration day because I was sick. Can I still register or change my courses, and what part of the procedure should I pay attention to?
samples.answer_text: If you cannot register on the registration day, you should complete the Late Registration during the registration change period [3]. After the registration change period ends, no registrations will be accepted for any reason, and those who do not complete registration during the designated time must take a leave of absence or withdraw from the university [2, 3, 4]. When making a registration change, you must click the "SAVE" button to finalize the procedure; otherwise, the process is not complete even if you input the data [2, 3, 4]. After registration or registration change, confirm that the courses you wish to take are reflected correctly [2, 3, 4].
samples.gold_answer: If you missed Registration Day, you should complete Late Registration during the registration change period; after that period, no registration is accepted for any reason. Complete the procedure with the SAVE button, confirm your added/cancelled courses in Registration Results, and receive your advisor's interview/online approval. Late Registration requires the 3,000 yen fee before advisor approval.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly states the late-registration window, no-registration-after-period rule, SAVE/results checks, advisor approval, and fee.

samples.question: If I want to withdraw from a course after seeing my workload, what rules or deadlines should I check before I make the decision?
samples.answer_text: If you wish to withdraw from the University, you should obtain the prescribed form, "Application for Withdrawal," from the Educational Affairs Group [3]. After completing outstanding bill payments, you must fill out the form, ensuring signatures from the Library, Financial Affairs Group, Student Affairs Group, your guarantor, and your academic advisor [3]. The completed form, along with your ID Card, must then be submitted to the Educational Affairs Group for the Dean's approval [3]. Specific official dates for withdrawal are: * **Spring Term:** August 31 [1] * **Autumn Term:** November 30 [1] * **Winter Term:** March 31 [1] Deadlines for submitting the application for withdrawal are: * **Spring Term:** March 31 [1] * **Autumn Term:** August 31 [1] * **Winter Term:** November 30 [1] If the stipulated date falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or national holiday, the deadline is the previous Friday [1]. If you are referring to withdrawing from *all* course registration for the ongoing term, this is called Registration Withdrawal. This must be submitted to the Educational Affairs Group with documents explaining the reasonable reasons, such as a medical certificate, and must be submitted one month prior to the last day of classes [2].
samples.gold_answer: Before deciding, check whether you are still inside the registration change period: after it ends, course-by-course changes are not accepted, and simply stopping attendance without dropping can result in an F. If you cannot continue because of illness or another unavoidable reason, Registration Withdrawal may be possible, but it withdraws all registered courses, not one course, and requires the stated application deadline/documentation. Approved withdrawn courses receive W and do not count in GPA.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly answers the drop/withdrawal distinction and the consequences after the registration change period.
samples.human_judge_notes: Focuses on university withdrawal and gives confusing term dates.

samples.question: I am trying to plan my major requirements and study abroad at the same time. Where should I look to confirm whether courses or credits will count toward my ICU requirements?
samples.answer_text: To confirm if courses or credits from study abroad or other institutions will count toward your ICU requirements, you should look into the specific guidelines for credit transfer. * **Study Abroad (Summer Abroad Programs):** Students who participated in approved summer programs and completed coursework at other universities may transfer credits if they are approved for ICU's graduation requirements. A maximum of 40 credits can be transferred, and these will count toward Elective Course requirements [1]. * **University Partners:** ICU students can take selected courses at Sophia University and have the credits counted toward graduation requirements through a review process [3, 4]. Similarly, students can take selected courses at the University of Tsukuba and have those credits counted toward graduation requirements through a review process [3, 4]. * **Transfer Students (General):** For courses taken at other universities before entering ICU, credits may be transferred if the courses are equivalent to ICU courses, are approved for ICU graduation requirements, and the grades received were 70 points or higher out of 100. However, the exact number and courses that are transferable will not be confirmed until after you complete the required procedures upon entering ICU [5].
samples.gold_answer: For study-abroad or other transfer credits, check the eHANDBOOK transfer-credit/study-abroad pages and the official study abroad materials. The retrieved evidence says credits count toward ICU graduation only after approval/review, with limits such as 40 credits for study abroad/summer abroad contexts and 60 credits for transfer students. It does not determine whether a specific course will count for your major requirement, so use the official credit-transfer procedure and Educational Affairs Group review for that confirmation.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence supports general transfer-credit review and limits, but not a major-specific decision for a particular course.
retrieved_passage_titles: University/Community Partners of ICU｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY
University/Community Partners of ICU｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY
Information on Transfer of Credits (for Transfer Student)｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY

samples.question: If I take a leave of absence and later return to ICU, what procedure or office information should I check before planning my courses again?
samples.answer_text: If you are returning after a leave of absence, you should check the following based on when you are returning: * **Returning from the 1st (Spring) semester:** The return period is from December 1st to the end of March. The application deadline is November 30th [1], [2]. * **Returning from the 2nd (Autumn) semester:** The return period is from April 1st to the end of August. The application deadline is March 31st [1], [2]. * **Returning from the 3rd (Winter) semester:** The return period is from September 1st to the end of November. The application deadline is August 31st [1], [2]. Additionally, if you do not hold a valid visa for residence in Japan and require a Certificate of Eligibility (CoE) for a "Study" visa, you should notify the university via email at gakuseki@icu.ac.jp for guidance on procedures [1], [2]. Please submit the application for the Certificate of Eligibility with plenty of time, as it typically takes about 1.5 to 2 months after applying to the Immigration Services Agency [1], [2]. If your leave of absence was due to illness, you must submit a doctor's certificate to the Academic Affairs Group proving you are in a health condition that does not impede your studies for the semester you wish to return [2]. If this is not submitted, you cannot participate in course registration [2].
samples.gold_answer: Before planning courses again, check the eHANDBOOK Leave of Absence and Reenrolment page and Educational Affairs Group information. Leave of absence uses a prescribed form with required signatures and deadlines; from AY2024 Autumn, the re-enrolment procedure itself is generally no longer required, but if the leave was for illness you must submit a physician's certificate to EAG by the stated deadline or you cannot participate in course registration. Non-Japanese citizens who need a student-visa CoE should email EAG at [gakuseki@icu.ac.jp](mailto:gakuseki@icu.ac.jp) early because the CoE process usually takes 1.5–2 months.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly covers leave procedures, reenrolment status, illness documentation, and CoE contact/timing.

ehandbook - 留学・休学・復学・退学・再入学・身分変更・身分延長・除籍

samples.question: I feel overwhelmed and want to talk to someone, but I am worried about privacy. Where can I find official information about counseling or student support at ICU?
samples.answer_text: You can find information regarding counseling, human rights consultation, health management, and support for students with disabilities through the following resources: * **Counseling, Human Rights Consultation, Health Management, and Disability Student Support:** ICU provides support for these areas [3]. * **ICU Counseling Center:** This center offers general mental health counseling, consultation about friends and career paths, and support for sharing feelings after a loss [1]. * **Health Care Office:** This office supports the health of students, faculty, and staff. Nurses provide first aid and health consultation daily, and the physician provides consultation and referral letters twice a week [2]. The Health Care Office also provides health management support for students and faculty, including first aid and health consultations from health staff, and annual health check-ups for all students and faculty [5]. * **Learning Accessibility Services (LAS) office:** This office provides reasonable accommodations for students with various disabilities, including physical, health, learning, developmental, and psychological disabilities [2], [5]. For more details on counseling, you can check the dedicated on-campus site [5].
samples.gold_answer: You can start with the ICU Health Care Office Mental Health page and ICU's Counseling, Human Rights Consultation and Health Services page. The evidence lists the ICU Counseling Center for general mental-health counseling and related concerns, and says the Counseling Center helps students organize problems while respecting the consultee's wishes. However, the retrieved evidence does not state confidentiality/privacy rules, so check the Counseling Center or internal site for those details.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence supports where to seek counseling help, but not the requested privacy/confidentiality details.

samples.question: If I need exam accommodations because of a disability or health condition, where should I start, and what kind of official support page should I look for?
samples.answer_text: To start if you need academic accommodations, you should first meet with a Learning Accessibility Services (LAS) staff member to discuss your needs [1]. After that, you must submit the required documents, which may include a medical certificate or a letter from a counselor or specialist, a Request for Academic Accommodations Form signed by your advisor, and any documentation of previous support received [1]. For more information specific to your needs, you should contact the LAS office [2]. The LAS office acts as an intermediary between you and an instructor to arrange accommodations for exams and assignments, such as extended exam time limits or arranging a separate exam room [2, 3].
samples.gold_answer: Start by contacting Learning Accessibility Services (LAS) and consulting your course instructor. The official support page to look for is CTL/LAS For Students or academic accommodations: the process includes meeting LAS staff, submitting medical/specialist documentation and a request form, developing an accommodation plan, and having LAS send requests to professors. Exam-related accommodations may include extended time, a separate room, scribes/typing support, or assistive laptops.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly identifies LAS, documentation steps, instructor consultation, and examples of exam accommodations.
samples.human_judge_notes: Covers LAS meeting, documents, intermediary role, and exam examples.

samples.question: I am graduating from undergraduate school but continuing to ICU graduate school. What should I know about my IT account or services after graduation?
samples.answer_text: If you are an undergraduate student graduating but continuing to ICU graduate school, new accounts will be issued after Matriculation day [4]. For students graduating, your account (including ICU Net ID, ICU Portal, Mail, and Google Workspace) will expire on March 31, 2026 [1]. If you need to keep exchanging emails after graduation, it is recommended that you use an email address that you can use after graduation, rather than a @icu.ac.jp email address, because email forwarding will stop [1]. You should also back up any necessary files from Gmail and Google Drive before graduation [1]. When your account is deleted, you will be unable to log in to Google (@icu.ac.jp) or view information such as the Joining ceremony announcement or Graduate school information [1].
samples.gold_answer: Your undergraduate ICU Net ID, ICU Portal, Mail, Google Workspace, and related services expire on March 31, 2026, and email forwarding also stops. This applies even if you are continuing from ICU undergraduate school to ICU graduate school, so back up needed Gmail/Drive files and switch necessary communication to another address before graduation. New accounts are issued after Matriculation Day; an optional alumni email provides Gmail and Google Calendar but not Google Drive.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly addresses account expiration, backup needs, applicability to ICU graduate entrants, and new account timing.

samples.question: 日本語プログラムを履修したいのですが、JLPのレベル分けや履修方法について、どこで公式情報を確認できますか？
samples.answer_text: JLPのレベル分けや履修方法に関する公式情報は、以下の情報源で確認できます。 * **JLPプログラムの詳細とコース提供について:** ICUのウェブサイトおよびJLPのウェブサイトを参照してください [2]。 * **JLPのレベル記述:** JLPは、各コースの学習目標をCEFR（Common European Framework of Reference for Languages）の指標を用いて記述しており、CEFRは言語能力をA1からC2までの6つのレベルに整理し、これらをBasic User、Independent User、Proficient Userの3つの大分類にまとめることができます [4]。 * **履修要件:** 9月入学の学生で日本語の知識がない場合、45単位分のJLPを履修する必要があります。日本語能力が高い場合は、JLPの履修要件の一部が免除されることがあります [5]。 特定の状況（例：ELA生がJLP科目を履修する場合）では、JLP Placement Test（PT）を受け、その結果によって履修できる科目レベルが決定されます [1]。 より詳細な情報については、JLPオフィス（ERB II Rm.104）に連絡するか、提供されているウェブサイトを参照してください [2]。
samples.gold_answer: JLPのレベルは、基本的にプレイスメントテストの結果で決まります。初回授業中にも日本語力の追加評価が行われ、その結果で履修レベルが変更される場合があります。公式情報はICUのJLPページ、JLPウェブサイト、該当するPortal告知で確認し、不明点はJLP Office（ERB II-104、[jlp@icu.ac.jp](mailto:jlp@icu.ac.jp)）に問い合わせてください。
samples.teacher_notes: 検索資料は、JLPのレベル決定方法、初回授業での再評価、公式情報源と問い合わせ先を直接示しています。
retrieved_passage_titles: ELA生のためのJLP科目について
Notes for graduate school students who wish to take the JLP courses
samples.human_judge_notes: Good sources but omits first-class reassessment and adds extra requirements.

samples.question: I remember there is a class called Statistical Information Literacy. How is the grade evaluated, and what kind of work does the syllabus seem to require?
samples.answer_text: The evaluation methods and required work for "Statistical Information Literacy" vary depending on the specific term and instructor: * **Spring 2026 (Instructor: KANEKO, Takuya):** The grading policy includes Exam (20%), Report (40%), Daily quiz (20%), and Class participation (20%) [1]. The course involves lectures on the basics of statistics, sampling methods, and data processing methods, and students use computers [4]. * **Autumn 2026 (Instructor: MARSZALEC, Daniel Janusz):** The grading policy includes Active participation in classes (15%), Apllication workshops (35%), and End of term data analysis project (50%) [2]. The course aims to teach basic data analysis skills for social sciences, covering statistics fundamentals, sampling methods, and data processing methods, with the use of computers [3].
samples.gold_answer: The grading depends on the section. In Spring 2026, the Japanese section is evaluated by Exam 20%, Report 40%, Daily quiz 20%, and Class participation 20%; in Autumn 2026, the English section is evaluated by Active participation 15%, Application workshops 35%, and an end-of-term data analysis project 50%. The syllabus descriptions suggest work in basic statistics, sampling/data processing, probability, distributions, sample data analysis, and computer/statistical-software-based data analysis.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence directly gives grading percentages and course-work topics for the relevant sections.
samples.human_judge_notes: Complete and term-specific.

samples.question: What are the mandatory courses I need to take after coming to ICU?
samples.regression_focus: English curriculum requirement query should not retrieve random single-course syllabi
samples.answer_text: After entering ICU, students study a curriculum where they do not decide on a major immediately; instead, they decide on a major at the end of the second year [1]. After enrollment, students study: * Language education subjects (English/Japanese) [1]. * General education subjects [1]. * Health and physical education subjects [1]. * Basic subjects provided by each major, which exceeds 30 in humanities and sciences [1]. Regarding general education subjects, they are taken over four years and involve mandatory courses such as "Introduction to Christianity" (キリスト教概論), which is the only required course for all students at ICU [2]. Students must complete a total of 18 units from general education subjects, which includes "Introduction to Christianity," courses from the humanities/social sciences, natural sciences, and "Liberal Arts Seminar" or "Special Lecture" courses [2]. The total required units for graduation is 130 units [3].
samples.gold_answer: The evidence gives a general answer but not a personalized list for your admissions route/proficiency. It identifies Introduction to Christianity as ICU's only required course for all students, and the PE graduation requirement as Health Science (1 credit) plus PE Exercise I, II, and III (together 1 credit). It also says your required language program is ELA or JLP depending on admissions route, and that language/specialized credit requirements vary, so check the official Graduation Requirements/eHANDBOOK for your own case.
samples.teacher_notes: The evidence supports key general requirements, but not a fully personalized mandatory-course list.

samples.question: 大学の卒論にチャットGPTを使っても大丈夫ですか？
samples.regression_focus: ChatGPT thesis policy should prefer official policy over newsletter-like matches
samples.answer_text: 生成系AIが作成したものを、成績評価のための提出物としてあたかも自分で作成したかのように使用することは、ICUのアカデミック・インテグリティーの方針が定める「剽窃」に該当します [1], [2]。 ICUのアカデミック・インテグリティーの方針では、「教員の評価を受けるための学生の提出物（レポート、試験の答案など）は、自分自身のもの（オリジナル作品）でなければなりません」と定められています [1], [2]。また、学生は自分で考え、調査研究したものでない情報の出典を明らかにし、正しく引用することが求められます [1], [2]。 生成系AIが使用するデータが他人の著作物や発表物から成り立っているため、AIが出力する回答自体が著作権侵害や剽窃である可能性も懸念されています [1], [2]。 生成系AIの使用を全面的に否定するものではありませんが、成績評価のための提出物として使用する場合は、ご自身のオリジナル作品である必要があります [1], [2]。
samples.gold_answer: 検索資料だけでは、卒論でChatGPTを使ってよいかを卒論専用ルールとして断定することはできません。ただしICUの学部方針では、生成系AIの使用を全面的に否定してはいない一方、生成系AIが作成したものを成績評価のための提出物に自分の作品のように使うことは剽窃にあたるとされています。自分で考え・調査したものではない情報は出典を明示して正しく引用し、AIの出力は不正確性・剽窃・プライバシー等のリスクを批判的に確認する必要があります。
samples.teacher_notes: 検索資料はICUの一般的な生成AI方針を示していますが、卒論専用の許可・禁止条件は示していません。
retrieved_passage_titles: 学部 教育方針｜国際基督教大学（ICU）

samples.human_judge_notes: Covers that AI use is not wholly denied but AI output cannot be submitted as one's own.

samples.question: Can I use ChatGPT for my senior thesis at ICU?
samples.regression_focus: English ChatGPT thesis policy should avoid over-answering from weak evidence
samples.answer_text: The unauthorized and unspecified use of generative AI in any stage of writing your senior thesis, and presenting work produced by others, including generative AI, as entirely your own, is considered a breach of ICU policy on academic integrity and will result in an E grade [1]. Students must also abide by the ICU Generative AI policies [1].
samples.gold_answer: You may not use ChatGPT or other generative AI for a senior thesis in an unauthorized or unspecified way. The Senior Thesis syllabus notes say students must obey ICU Generative AI policies, and that unauthorized/unspecified generative-AI use at any stage of writing the senior thesis, or presenting work produced by others including generative AI as entirely your own, breaches Academic Integrity and receives an E grade. Evidence therefore supports use only when it is authorized/specified and policy-compliant.
samples.teacher_notes: The Senior Thesis syllabus evidence directly states the generative-AI rule and consequence.
samples.human_judge_notes: States the unauthorized/unspecified generative-AI rule and E-grade consequence.

samples.question: What are the mandatory courses I need to take after coming to ICU?

samples.question: Can I use ChatGPT for my senior thesis at ICU?
samples.regression_focus: English ChatGPT thesis policy should avoid over-answering from weak evidence
samples.answer_text: You may not use ChatGPT or other generative AI for a senior thesis in an unauthorized or unspecified way. The Senior Thesis syllabus notes say students must obey ICU Generative AI policies, and that unauthorized/unspecified generative-AI use at any stage of writing the senior thesis, or presenting work produced by others including generative AI as entirely your own, breaches Academic Integrity and receives an E grade. Evidence therefore supports use only when it is authorized/specified and policy-compliant.
